Setting goals is the first step in turning the invisible into the visible. — Tony Robbins
An inventor is a man who asks Why? of the universe and lets nothing stand between the answer and his mind. — Ayn Rand
The road to success is always under construction.. — Lily Tomlin
When you want to be honored by others, you learn to honor them first. — Sathya Sai Baba
Never was anything great achieved without danger. — Niccolo Machiavelli
It is the supreme art of the teacher to awaken joy in creative expression and knowledge. — Albert Einstein
The most successful people are those who are good at plan B. — James A. Yorke
I hear and I forget. I see and I remember. I do and I understand. — Confucius
Knowing is not enough; we must apply. Willing is not enough; we must do. — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
Sustaining true friendship is a lot more challenging than we give it credit for. — Mariella Frostrup
How people treat you is their karma; how you react is yours. — Wayne Dyer
A clever person turns great troubles into little ones, and little ones into none at all. — Chinese Proverb
All men are equal before fish. — Herbert Hoover
You play the hand you're dealt. I think the game's worthwhile. — Christopher Reeve
There is an essence of when you are fearless, you become more creative. — Gurbaksh Chahal
There is no retirement for an artist, it's your way of living so there is no end to it. — Henry Moore
The real problem is not whether machines think but whether men do. — B. F. Skinner
One that desires to excel should endeavor in those things that are in themselves most excellent. — Epictetus
Look back over the past, with its changing empires that rose and fell, and you can foresee the future, too. — Marcus Aurelius
Life is just a chance to grow a soul. — A. Powell Davies
We are all here for some special reason. — Robin Sharma
It is better to disappoint people with the truth than to appease them with a lie. — Simon Sinek
If you don't have time to do it right, when will you have time to do it over? — John Wooden
The best answer to anger is silence. — Marcus Aurelius
Success is within the reach of just about everyone. — Lolly Daskal
If A is success in life, then A equals x plus y plus z. Work is x; y is play; and z is keeping your mouth shut. — Albert Einstein
Real success is finding your lifework in the work that you love. — David McCullough
He who is contented is rich. — Laozi
I'd rather regret the things that I have done than the things that I have not done. — Lucille Ball
Any sufficiently advanced technology is equivalent to magic. — Arthur C. Clarke
The sweetest pleasure arises from difficulties overcome. — Publilius Syrus
As you grow older you will discover that you have two hands. One for helping yourself, the other for helping others. — Audrey Hepburn
People do not decide their futures, they decide their habits and their habits decide their futures. — Gary Keller
You're always free to change your mind and choose a different future, or a different past. — Richard Bach
If you realize that you're the problem, then you can change yourself, learn something and grow wiser. Don't blame other people for your problems. — Robert Kiyosaki
By accepting yourself and being fully what you are, your presence can make others happy. — Jane Roberts
If you lose today, win tomorrow. In this never-ending spirit of challenge is the heart of a victor. — Daisaku Ikeda
A true friend is one who overlooks your failures and tolerates your success! — Doug Larson
Watch the little things; a small leak will sink a great ship. — Benjamin Franklin
Be patient and calm; no one can catch fish in anger. — Herbert Hoover
Believe in your infinite potential. Your only limitations are those you set upon yourself. — Roy T. Bennett
Success is never final; failure is never fatal. — Conrad Hilton
Whenever you do a thing, act as if all the world were watching. — Thomas Jefferson
The greatest of all mistakes is to do nothing because you think you can only do a little. — Zig Ziglar
If one is lucky, a solitary fantasy can totally transform one million realities. — Maya Angelou
Perfection of means and confusion of ends seems to characterize our age. — Albert Einstein
If a man going down into a river, swollen and swiftly flowing, is carried away by the current — how can he help others across? – The Buddha — The Buddha
Accept challenges, so that you may feel the exhilaration of victory. — George S. Patton
Until you make peace with who you are, you'll never be content with what you have. — Doris Mortman
What a liberation to realize that 'the voice in my head' is not who I am. Who am I then? The one who sees that. — Eckhart Tolle
The best way to predict your future is to create it. — Abraham Lincoln
Monsters are real, and ghosts are real too. They live inside us, and sometimes, they win. — Stephen King
Many roads lead to the path, but basically there are only two: reason and practice. — Bodhidharma
Discipline yourself, and others won't need to. — John Wooden
Lots of people want to ride with you in the limo, but what you want is someone who will take the bus with you when the limo breaks down. — Oprah Winfrey
Without leaps of imagination, or dreaming, we lose the excitement of possibilities. Dreaming, after all, is a form of planning. — Gloria Steinem
The eye sees only what the mind is prepared to comprehend. — Henri Bergson
Listen to what you know instead of what you fear. — Richard Bach
Never apologize for showing feeling. When you do so, you apologize for truth. — Benjamin Disraeli
I've always lived in the moment. — Yanni
You can easily judge the character of a man by how he treats those who can do nothing for him. — Simon Sinek
Everybody has talent, but ability takes hard work. — Michael Jordan
The enemy is a very good teacher. — Dalai Lama
Wherever you are is the entry point. — Kabir
I care not so much what I am to others as what I am to myself. I will be rich by myself, and not by borrowing. — Michel de Montaigne
Four steps to achievement: Plan purposefully. Prepare prayerfully. Proceed positively. Pursue persistently. — William Arthur Ward
Knowledge is going to make you stronger. Knowledge is going to let you control your life. Knowledge is going to give you the wisdom to teach their children. Knowledge is the thing that makes you smile in the face of disaster. — Avery Brooks
Ever tried. Ever failed. No matter. Try Again. Fail again. Fail better. — Samuel Beckett
It is in your moments of decision that your destiny is shaped. — Tony Robbins
In a world that is constantly changing, it is to our advantage to learn how to adapt and enjoy something better. — Spencer Johnson
Take care of your body. It's the only place you have to live. — Jim Rohn
Laziness may appear attractive, but work gives satisfaction. — Anne Frank
Turn the pain into power. — Unknown
Remain calm. Be kind. — Colin Powell
Wisdom is the right use of knowledge. To know is not to be wise. Many men know a great deal and are all the greater fools for it. There is no fool so great a fool as a knowing fool. But to know how to use knowledge is to have wisdom. — Charles Spurgeon
That's the risk you take if you change: that people you've been involved with won't like the new you. But other people who do will come along. — Lisa Alther
When you see a man of worth, think of how you may emulate him. When you see one who is unworthy, examine yourself. — Confucius
To lead people walk behind them. — Laozi
Setting an example is not the main means of influencing another, it is the only means. — Albert Einstein
What the caterpillar calls the end of the world, the master calls a butterfly. — Richard Bach
Blessed is he who expects nothing, for he shall never be disappointed. — Alexander Pope
Do not dwell in the past, do not dream of the future, concentrate the mind on the present moment. — Buddha
Just throw away all thoughts of imaginary things, and stand firm in that which you are. — Kabir
We take care of the future best by taking care of the present now. — Jon Kabat-Zinn
Without some goals and some efforts to reach it, no man can live. — John Dewey
Where there is love there is life. — Mahatma Gandhi
Do the one thing you think you cannot do. Fail at it. Try again. Do better the second time. The only people who never tumble are those who never mount the high wire. This is your moment. Own it. — Oprah Winfrey
It is more shameful to distrust our friends than to be deceived by them. — Confucius
Faith, as well intentioned as it may be, must be built on facts, not fiction--faith in fiction is a damnable false hope. — Thomas Edison
